page: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on the x86_64 architecture. The configuration allocates 48 physical cores with a single thread per core on a shared CPU allocation model, running at a base speed of 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 400 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for raw computational power. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance rank in the top 10% of tested systems, mirrored by a PassMark CPU score of 84358.21. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are also highly rated, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 43.07 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieving 640.54 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is strong, registering 261.26 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 109.87 GB/sec for write operations.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads rather than memory-intensive databases. It demonstrates strong database performance with 25075.42 PassMark database operations and 77.39 million Redis SET operations per second. It is also capable of handling lightweight machine learning tasks, processing 4575.29 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M LLM model, though text generation speeds for larger models are average. This profile is highly suitable for parallel processing, web serving, and compute-bound applications.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 48 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9575F processor. Running at 2.0 GHz with a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is tailored for compute-intensive workloads. It includes a bundled 400 GB SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data highlights top-tier CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics ranking in the top 10% of systems. Memory bandwidth is strong, delivering 261.26 GB/sec for large-block reads, while database operations reach 25075.42 on PassMark. Although memory capacity is moderate relative to the high core count, the server excels in parallel processing, compression tasks, and high-throughput web serving, making it an efficient choice for compute-heavy applications.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, delivering top-tier CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-96GB features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, offering top-tier compute performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and bundled SSD storage for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
96.0 GB system memory and bundled 400 GB SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput web serving
Best suited for parallel processing, compression, and compute-heavy workloads
